name: Magenta UFO Crash - Italy 1933
note: The 1933 Magenta, Italy crash - Italy's pre-Roswell crash retrieval claim; documents Mussolini's alleged RS/33 cabinet under Marconi, the anonymous-source Italian document set, and David Grusch's 2023 sworn congressional testimony naming it as the earliest U.S.-acquired non-human craft.

name: Aurora Airship Crash
note: The 1897 Aurora, Texas airship crash - the earliest claimed crash-recovery event in the DECUR database, based on S.E. Haydon's Dallas Morning News dispatch; thoroughly documented with MUFON 1973 investigation findings, 2008 GPR survey results, and full hoax-vs-genuine competing hypotheses analysis.

name: Monica Jacinto Reza
note: JPL rocket scientist and Mondaloy superalloy co-inventor who disappeared June 2025; her institutional connection to Maj. Gen. Neil McCasland through AFRL-funded Mondaloy development - and McCasland's own February 2026 disappearance - places her at the center of the White House investigation into missing advanced aerospace researchers.

name: Amy Eskridge
note: Anti-gravity researcher and Institute for Exotic Science co-founder who died in June 2022; the only figure in the 2022-2026 missing scientists cluster named directly in congressional UAP testimony, with journalist Michael Shellenberger asserting she was murdered by a private aerospace company due to her involvement in UAP research.

name: Boyd Bushman
note: Senior Research Scientist at Lockheed Martin whose 2014 deathbed interview claimed knowledge of alien beings at Area 51; notable for genuine aerospace credentials alongside the debunking of his alien photographs as commercially available toy figurines.

name: Technology of Unknown Origins
note: New glossary entry for TUO - the institutional term for recovered hardware of unattributable origin, used in Congressional hearings and DoD contexts as an epistemically neutral alternative to extraterrestrial or non-human framing.

name: Die Glocke
note: New glossary entry for Die Glocke - the alleged Nazi Bell device described by Igor Witkowski and popularized by Nick Cook, frequently cited in UAP reverse-engineering literature as a potential precursor to post-war anti-gravity programs.

name: Slowwalker
note: New glossary entry for Slowwalker - the NORAD counterpart to Fastwalker, designating anomalous objects moving too slowly at altitude to be explained by conventional orbital mechanics.

name: Alien Reproduction Vehicle
note: New glossary entry for the ARV - Mark McCandlish's term for alleged human-built reverse-engineered craft, with updated ARV abbreviation entry cross-referencing the full term.

name: Lubbock Lights
note: August-September 1951 multi-witness case featuring authenticated photographs by Carl Hart Jr., corroborating radar evidence from Reese AFB, testimony from four Texas Tech professors, and an official Project Blue Book Unknown classification.

name: Voronezh UFO Incident
note: September 1989 Soviet mass-witness landing case notable for being officially reported by TASS state news agency, with physical trace evidence documented by militia and scientists at Comintern Park in Voronezh.

name: John Podesta
note: Former White House Chief of Staff to Clinton and Counselor to Obama, notable for publicly stating his biggest failure of 2014 was not securing UAP file disclosure and for writing the foreword to Leslie Kean's landmark 2010 UAP book.

name: Leonard Stringfield
note: Pioneering crash retrieval researcher who compiled testimony from over 100 anonymous military and medical sources across seven Status Reports (1978-1994), establishing the foundational archive for all subsequent crash retrieval investigation.

name: Ralph Blumenthal
note: Veteran New York Times investigative reporter who co-authored the landmark December 2017 AATIP article with Leslie Kean and Helene Cooper, widely credited with launching the modern UAP disclosure era.

name: Dr. Robert Sarbacher
note: MIT-trained physicist and R&D Board consultant whose 1950 meeting with Wilbert Smith and 1983 written letter to researcher William Steinman make him one of the few credentialed U.S. scientists to leave a documented record corroborating crash recovery program knowledge.

name: Ben Rich
note: Second director of Lockheed Skunk Works and F-117 program lead; notable for a documented 1986 letter and reported 1993 UCLA speech suggesting classified U.S. programs possessed interstellar-capable propulsion technology.

name: UAP Sightings Database
note: New /sightings page integrating 614,505 UFOSINT community reports with interactive heatmap, temporal chart, breakdown charts, and case cross-references.

name: Nick Cook
note: Aviation journalist and Jane's Defence Weekly editor; investigated classified aerospace programs and their UAP connections.

name: Charles Hall
note: Former U.S. Air Force weather observer at Nellis AFB; author of the Millennial Hospitality series describing alleged contact with 'Tall White' extraterrestrials.

name: Dan Sherman
note: Former NSA signals intelligence specialist who claims to have served as an 'intuitive communicator' in a classified program called Project Preserve Destiny.

name: Dr. Salvatore Pais
note: Naval Air Station Patuxent River aerospace engineer behind controversial patents for a 'hybrid aerospace-underwater craft' using high-energy electromagnetic fields.

name: Donna Hare
note: Former NASA contractor at Johnson Space Center who testified to witnessing UFO photographs being airbrushed out of official NASA imagery.

name: Jonathan Weygandt
note: Former U.S. Marine who testified to encountering a crashed non-human craft in Peru in 1997 and being subsequently intimidated into silence.

name: Bob Jacobs
note: Former U.S. Air Force lieutenant who filmed a UFO disabling a test warhead over Vandenberg AFB in 1964 before being ordered to deny the incident.

name: Mark McCandlish
note: Technical illustrator and aerospace researcher who claimed to have detailed knowledge of a classified anti-gravity vehicle called the Alien Reproduction Vehicle.

name: Parviz Jafari
note: Iranian Air Force general who piloted the F-4 interceptor during the landmark 1976 Tehran UFO Incident, where weapons systems failed on approach.

name: Oscar Santa Maria Huerta
note: Peruvian Air Force pilot who engaged a UAP with 30mm cannon fire over La Joya Air Base in 1980; rounds had no effect on the object.

name: Clifford Stone
note: Retired U.S. Army sergeant who testified to participating in UFO crash retrieval operations over a 22-year career and claimed knowledge of a 57-species catalogue.

name: John Ramirez
note: Retired CIA analyst (GS-15) who worked in the Directorate of Science and Technology; has spoken publicly about UAP-related information compartmentalization.

name: Foo Fighters - WWII Aerial Phenomena
note: Documented reports of luminous objects observed by Allied and Axis pilots from 1944-1945; investigated post-war by multiple governments.

name: Trans-en-Provence Landing
note: Investigated by GEPAN; physical trace evidence confirmed by laboratory analysis - one of the most rigorously studied ground-trace cases on record.

name: Cash-Landrum Incident
note: Two civilians and a child suffered acute radiation injuries after a close encounter in Texas; subsequently sued the U.S. government for damages.

name: Travis Walton Abduction
note: Seven-man logging crew witnessed Walton struck by a beam of light and taken; he reappeared five days later. Six crew members passed polygraph examinations.

name: Betty and Barney Hill Abduction
note: First widely-reported alien abduction case in the U.S.; hypnotic regression produced consistent corroborating accounts between the two witnesses.

name: Travis Walton
note: Arizona logger and central figure in the 1975 Snowflake abduction; passed multiple polygraph tests and authored Fire in the Sky.

name: Budd Hopkins
note: Pioneering abduction researcher and artist who documented hundreds of experiencer accounts and founded the Intruders Foundation.

name: Michael Herrera
note: Former U.S. Marine who testified to encountering a massive octagonal craft during a 2009 humanitarian mission in Indonesia, operated by private military personnel.

name: Gov. Fife Symington
note: Former Governor of Arizona who initially mocked the 1997 Phoenix Lights publicly, then recanted in 2007 and confirmed witnessing an enormous structured craft.

name: James Fox
note: Documentary filmmaker behind I Know What I Saw and The Phenomenon; produced some of the most credible witness-testimony footage in the UAP genre.

name: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
note: Florida congresswoman and vocal advocate for UAP transparency; co-sponsored legislation requiring government disclosure of non-human intelligence data.

name: Dr. Lynne Kitei
note: Phoenix physician and researcher who personally witnessed and photographed the 1997 Phoenix Lights; produced documentary on the incident.

name: Maj. Gen. Neil McCasland
note: Former head of Air Force Research Laboratory; reportedly confirmed to Tom DeLonge that U.S. government has recovered non-human craft.

name: Ingo Swann
note: Psychic and artist whose early remote viewing demonstrations led directly to the CIA's development of the Stargate Program.

name: Joseph McMoneagle
note: Stargate Program remote viewer (RV001); widely considered the most accurate operational viewer in the program's history.

name: Dr. Beatriz Villarroel
note: Swedish astrophysicist leading the VASCO project, which searches archival astronomical data for anomalous transient objects of potential non-natural origin.

name: Dr. Kevin Knuth
note: Former NASA researcher and University of Albany physics professor who has published peer-reviewed analysis estimating UAP performance characteristics.

name: Jake Barber
note: Former special operations officer and contractor who testified to participating in UAP crash retrieval operations and claims direct knowledge of NHI programs.

name: Dr. Timothy Gallaudet
note: Retired U.S. Navy rear admiral and former NOAA administrator; has publicly stated that USO phenomena represent a significant national security gap.
